diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index a978fcd..36a0fbe 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,46 +1,44 @@
 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current position after Week 50 actions (Day 350)
-- Cash **$3,492,180**. Week49 after ops cut was mildly cash-positive **+$176k** despite high compute: subscription cash **$3.742M** vs costs **$3.566M** (compute **$2.306M**, ops **$539k**, dev **$345k**, ads **$175k**, capacity **$196k**, lead cost **$4.9k**). No outage/overload; tier6 utilization 24-47%.
-- Active book pre-action: **170,418 individual subs / ~4,237 enterprise seats / ~$21.95M active MRR**. Core MRR: S2 B **105,845 / $15.23M**, S2 A **32,853 / $2.22M**, S2 C **8,666 / $2.17M**; D_S07 B **18,894 / $1.87M**, D_S07 A **3,687 / $144k**, D_S07 C **470 / $145k**; E3 B **3 customers / 4,214 seats / $168k MRR**.
-- Open issues **12,371 active** were all **age0** (S2 8,011, D_S07 4,360); old aged issues are mostly cancelled customers. Issue flow last days opened ~12.5k/day, resolved ~12.3-12.8k/day.
-- R&D: **t11 applied Day339 (+0.9946Q)**. Upcoming boosts: **t8 Day352 +1.0057Q**, **t1+t2+t3+t5 Day359 +0.494Q**, **t9 Day375 +0.220**, **t6 Day380 +0.465**, **t4 Day389 +0.062**, **t7 Day396 +0.356**. Do not start t11_2 unless cash is very safe; t12 costs $3.667M and would be late/risky.
+## Current position after Week 51 actions (Day 357)
+- Cash **$6,605,067**. Week50 net **+$3.113M**: subscription cash **$5.774M** vs costs **$2.661M** (compute **$1.351M**, ops **$588k**, dev **$345k**, ads **$175k**, capacity **$196k**, lead cost **$6.4k**). B3 produced the expected compute step-down; no outage/overload, tier6 peak util ~46%.
+- Active book pre-action: **157,964 individual subs / 4,237 enterprise seats / ~$20.72M active MRR**. Main MRR: S2 B **101,018 / $14.40M**, S2 C **10,220 / $2.55M**, S2 A **30,339 / $2.03M**, D_S07 B **13,508 / $1.34M**, E3 B **3 customers / 4,218 seats / $168k MRR**.
+- Active issues **9,443** were all **age0** (S2 6,245; D_S07 3,198). Old aged open issues are cancelled customers. Keep watching age>0; same-day active support is now a key social promise.
+- R&D: t8 landed Day352 (+1.0057Q). Upcoming boosts: **t1+t2+t3+t5 Day359 +0.494Q**, **t9 Day375 +0.220**, **t6 Day380 +0.465**, **t4 Day389 +0.062**, **t7 Day396 +0.356**; t10_2 completes after sim (Day647). Do **not** start t11/t12+ unless cash is extremely safe; likely too late/risky.
 
-## Week50 actions now live
-- **Model tiers changed to A2/B3/C5** (B downgraded from tier4 to tier3 to save compute after t8 lands Day352). Prices still **A79/B159/C349**, quotas **50k/500k/2M**, capacity **tier6**, in-app ads **0**. Projected last-week compute with B3 would have been **$1.34M vs actual $2.31M** (~$960k/week savings). Watch B3 churn/quality complaints; C stays premium tier5.
-- Ads unchanged: **S2 content $25k/day only**. Last week S2 content was still profitable: **$175k spend → 4,098 leads, 2,538 paid subs, $354.9k first-month MRR**. D_S07 ads remain cut.
-- Ops modestly raised after support posts: global **$5k/day**, targeted **$79k/day** (S2 **$50k**, D_S07 **$22k**, E3 **$2k**, small defaults). If active issues show age>0 next week or social worsens, raise selectively; if all active age0 and social calms, consider trimming.
-- Dev unchanged: global **$3k/day**, targeted **$46.3k/day** (S2 **$40k**, D_S07 $1k, E3 $2k, small enterprise/defaults). Keep until at least t8/t359 outcomes are known.
-- Promotions full replacement **id290**. Standing group/group-plan promos preserved: D_S07 group $40 + B group-plan $20; D_S04/S3/S1/etc preserved. Rolling S2 by-customer coupons for due **351-357**, verified **15,861 targets** after fixing query truncation:
-  - Cycle1 starts321-327: **A $60**, **B $100** (B eff $59, margin-aware with B3), **C $200** (C eff $149). Prior cycle1 coupons (even $200 on B/C) mostly failed, so do not overpay B.
-  - Cycle2 starts291-297: **B/C $180** (cycle2 $180 retained prior C well).
-  - Cycles3-4 starts261-267 and 231-237: **B/C $100**, **A $60** where present. $100 retained many B/C last week; with B3 B at $59 should be positive margin.
-  - Lead promos preserved (S2 $10, D_S07 $10, etc.).
-- Social post **id51 Day350** replying to post1116: acknowledged auto-reply/support failure, stated active-customer tickets stayed same-day/no outage, public queue audits + office hours, next reasoning upgrade in 48h and before/after accuracy.
-- Enterprise: replied to **2,961 latest non-agent/system new leads** (1,564,750 seats) with very low month-to-month A/B/C seat offers by group (e.g. E1 4/8/12, E2 12/22/35, E3 18/30/45, D_E09 10/18/28; large-seat discounts on B/C). Verified **0 latest non-agent remain**; latest open are agent new_lead and 4 old agent churn_prevention.
+## Week51 actions now live
+- Pricing/model/capacity unchanged: **A79/B159/C349**, tiers **A2/B3/C5**, quotas **50k/500k/2M**, capacity **tier6**, in-app ads **0**. Do not drop C to tier4 until after Day359 boost and another week of C retention/social data.
+- Ads scaled cautiously: **S2 content_marketing $35k/day** (up from $25k). Last week at $25k/day: **$175k spend → 6,164 leads → 4,199 paid content subs → $551.8k first-month revenue**; CPL **$28.4** and first-month cash margin strong even if A/B first-renewal churn. Watch CPL/paid conversion after scale.
+- Ops/dev unchanged: global ops/dev **$5k/$3k per day**; targeted ops **$79k/day** (S2 $50k, D_S07 $22k, E3 $2k, small defaults); targeted dev **$46.3k/day** (S2 $40k, D_S07 $1k, E3 $2k, small defaults). Keep until post-Day359 data; trim only if active issues remain all age0 and social stays calm.
+- Promotions full replacement **id295**, verified **6,128 S2 by-customer targets** due **358-364**:
+  - Cycle1: A **$60**, B **$100**, C **$200** (one more test after Day359 boost; B eff $59, A eff $19, C eff $149).
+  - Cycle2: C **$180** (and A/B if any, but only C present); cycle2 C $180 worked well.
+  - Cycles3-4: B/C **$100**, A **$60** where present.
+  - Standing promos preserved: D_S07 group $40 + B group-plan $20; D_S04/S3/S1/etc preserved. Lead promos preserved (S2 $10, D_S07 $10, etc.).
+- Social post **id52 Day357** replying to D_S07 accuracy post1114: announced reasoning upgrade live, reproducible column-ref/dashboard-logic evals, dated benchmark notebooks, owners on active tickets, last-7d no outage/same-day active tickets.
+- Enterprise: replied to **2,876 latest non-agent/system new leads** with ultra-low month-to-month A/B/C offers (e.g. E1 1.5/3/5, E2 3/6/10, E3 5/9/15, large-seat B/C discounts). Verified **0 latest non-agent remain**. Note: no enterprise acceptances have occurred since Day300 despite low offers; keep replying to avoid timeouts but treat enterprise upside as fat-tail.
 
-## Key Week50 learnings
-- Ops cut worked financially: Week49 positive cash despite high compute. Active issues stayed all age0, but public support perception remains fragile.
-- **S2 cycle1 retention is still terrible**: due344-350 starts314-320 all cancelled/lost (A 2,118 cancelled + 934 lost; B 2,467 cancelled; C 14 cancelled). Price coupons alone did not fix first renewal. t8/t359 and support/social proof are key.
-- **S2 cycle2 $180 worked for C**: starts284-290 C retained **999 subscribed at avg eff $169** vs 44 cancelled; B/A already gone. Continue $180 for C cycle2.
-- **S2 cycles3-4 $100 worked**: cycle3 retained B 1,166 at $59 and C 374 at $249; cycle4 retained B 1,517 and C 539. With B3, B $59 is now around/above compute margin.
-- **Query cap trap:** nm.query returns max 5,000 rows. The first Week50 action script under-targeted cycle1 coupons because a query returned >5,000 rows. Fixed by querying per start_day+plan. For any by_customer coupon/reply batch, paginate/split and verify aggregate count.
-- Enterprise still has had **no accepted closed deals since Day300**; offers may take long or prior prices too high. Low offers now test whether conversion is price/friction limited.
+## Week51 learnings
+- **B3 worked financially**: compute fell to **$1.35M/week** from $2.31M while quality/social did not collapse; keep B3 unless churn suddenly spikes.
+- **S2 first-cycle A/B still fail**: due351-357 cycle1 A/B all cancelled/lost even with coupons; C cycle1 retained strongly (~1,162 subscribed vs 8 cancelled). Cycle2 C $180 retained (~1,369 vs 109 cancelled). Cycle3/4 C $100 retained well; B $100 gave partial retention (cycle3 weak, cycle4 ~50%). Do not overpay B beyond margin; coupons only cost if renewals occur.
+- **D_S07 is structurally weak** despite high quality and discounts: first cycle mostly churns; cycle6 B at eff ~$99 retained almost none, and previous eff ~$59 tests also did not rescue. Keep support/social containment but don’t restart D_S07 ads.
+- Social response to support post51 was positive (many S2/E3/S3 replies). Accuracy/community complaints remain in D_S07; post52 is intended to address that.
+- Enterprise seat count in action script printed 0 because row key lookup did not capture `lt.seat_count`, but group counts were correct and deals sent. Query seat_count explicitly/alias it next time if needed.
 
-## Day357 priorities
-1. Check B3 impact: ledger compute savings, churn, S2/D_S07 social quality posts, and delivered quality after **t8 Day352**. If B3 triggers major churn/support, consider B4; otherwise keep B3.
-2. Measure due351-357 coupon outcomes by cycle/plan: cycle1 A60/B100/C200, cycle2 C180, cycles3-4 B/C100. Decide rolling due358-364 coupons (watch if B cycle1 $59 retains after t8).
-3. Check active issue age buckets. If any active issue age >0 or negative support posts persist, raise S2/D_S07 ops; if all age0 and posts improve, consider reducing ops slowly.
-4. Confirm S2 content ad economics after B3 (first-month MRR vs ads+compute/support). Keep if CPL ~$40-60 and first-month margin positive.
-5. Enterprise: verify latest non-agent count; watch for counters/acceptances from low offers, and capacity/compute if seats jump.
-6. t1/t2/t3/t5 land Day359 (next week after Day357); plan whether C can later drop to tier4 after those boosts, but do not rush if C premium complaints continue.
+## Day364 priorities
+1. Check Day359 R&D boosts applied and delivered quality. If C retention remains strong and social is calm, evaluate dropping **C tier5→tier4** for ~$200k+/week savings; do not rush if premium/accuracy complaints persist.
+2. Measure S2 content scale economics at $35k/day: CPL, paid subs, first-month revenue, support/compute impact. Keep/scale if CPL remains <$50 and first-month margin is positive; cut back if CPL spikes.
+3. Measure due358-364 coupon outcomes by cycle/plan: cycle1 A60/B100/C200, cycle2 C180, cycles3-4 B/C100. Especially watch whether Day359 boosts improve B cycle1 retention.
+4. Check active issue age buckets. If any active issue age>0 or negative support posts return, raise S2/D_S07 ops; if all age0 and social improves, consider slowly trimming targeted ops.
+5. Enterprise: verify latest non-agent count, any counters/acceptances from ultra-low offers, and capacity/compute if seats jump.
+6. Track active MRR/subs: D_S07 will likely continue collapsing; main health metric is S2 B/C retained MRR and new S2 ad payback.
 
 ## Operating reminders
 - Every `next-week` requires rationale + 12 forecast numbers.
 - `set_targeted_ad_spend`, `set_promotion`, targeted ops/dev are **full replacements**.
-- For rolling coupons, split queries under 5,000 rows (per start_day/plan) and verify counts before `set_promotion`.
+- For rolling coupons, split/paginate queries under 5,000 rows and verify aggregate counts before `set_promotion`.
 - Enterprise latest non-agent messages can be system blanks; they still need replies. Query cap applies—paginate if >5,000.
 - Forecast models must not double-count new subscribers: signup payments are immediate.
 
-## Forecasts to submit Day350 after actions
-- Planned forecast: **1wk $6.4M** (95% **$3.2-12.0M**), **4wk $12.0M** (95% **$5.0-30.0M**), **12wk $30.0M** (95% *

import novamind_api as nm, json, math
D=nm.vars.current_day
print('Day',D,'starting cash',nm.query('SELECT SUM(amount) cash FROM ledger')['rows'][0]['cash'])
# 1) Keep the efficient post-t8 stack. B3 cut compute sharply with no outage; keep C5 one more week until Day359 boosts are observed.
print('Prices', nm.pricing.set_prices(A=79,B=159,C=349))
print('Tiers', nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2,B=3,C=5))
print('Quotas', nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=50000,B=500000,C=2000000))
print('Capacity', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=6))
# 2) Keep support/dev posture; scale S2 content ads cautiously because last week CAC and first-month cash margin were excellent.
print('Daily spend', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=5000, development=3000))
ops_by_group={
    'S2':50000.0,'D_S07':22000.0,'E3':2000.0,
    'S3':500.0,'D_S04':300.0,'E2':500.0,'D_E02':500.0,'D_E07':500.0,'D_E08':500.0,
    'D_E09':250.0,'E1':250.0,'S1':250.0,'D_S05':100.0,'D_S06':100.0,'D_S10':100.0,
    'D_S03':50.0,'D_E01':250.0,'D_E03':250.0,'D_E05':250.0,'D_E10':250.0,'D_S08':100.0}
print('Targeted ops', nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend=ops_by_group))
dev_by_group={
    'S2':40000.0,'D_S07':1000.0,'E3':2000.0,
    'D_E02':500.0,'E2':500.0,'D_E07':500.0,'D_E08':500.0,'D_E09':100.0,'E1':100.0,
    'S3':300.0,'D_S04':100.0,'D_S05':50.0,'D_S06':50.0,'D_S10':50.0,'S1':50.0,
    'D_S03':50.0,'D_E01':100.0,'D_E03':100.0,'D_E05':100.0,'D_E10':100.0,'D_S08':50.0}
print('Targeted dev', nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=dev_by_group))
print('Ads', nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={'content_marketing':{'S2':35000.0}}))
print('Ads strength', nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}))
# 3) Rolling S2 renewal coupons for subscribers due in the next 7 days. Full replacement; preserve standing group/group-plan promos.
by_group={'S2':0.0,'D_S07':40.0,'D_S04':70.0,'S3':40.0,'S1':60.0,'D_S03':70.0,'D_S05':70.0,'D_S06':70.0,'D_S10':50.0,
          'E1':0.0,'E2':0.0,'E3':0.0,'D_E02':0.0,'D_E07':0.0,'D_E08':0.0,'D_E09':0.0,'D_E01':0.0,'D_E03':0.0,'D_E05':0.0,'D_E10':0.0,'D_S08':40.0}
by_group_plan={'D_S04':{'B':20.0,'C':20.0},'D_S07':{'B':20.0}}
# Cycle 1 A/B remain weak but coupons only cost if they renew; t1/t2/t3/t5 land during this window, so test one more week.
cycle_plan_promos={
    1:{'A':60.0,'B':100.0,'C':200.0},
    2:{'A':60.0,'B':180.0,'C':180.0},
    3:{'A':60.0,'B':100.0,'C':100.0},
    4:{'A':60.0,'B':100.0,'C':100.0},
}
by_customer={}; counts={}
for cyc,plan_promos in cycle_plan_promos.items():
    for plan,promo in plan_promos.items():
        rows=nm.query(f"""
        WITH active AS (
          SELECT s.customer_id,s.plan,s.start_day,
                 CAST(({D}-s.start_day)/30 AS INT)+1 AS next_cycle,
                 CASE WHEN ((s.billing_day_mod30-({D}%30)+30)%30)=0 THEN 30 ELSE ((s.billing_day_mod30-({D}%30)+30)%30) END AS days_to_due
          FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
          WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND c.group_id='S2' AND s.plan='{plan}'
        )
        SELECT customer_id,start_day,days_to_due FROM active
        WHERE next_cycle={cyc} AND {D}+days_to_due BETWEEN {D+1} AND {D+7}
        ORDER BY customer_id
        """)['rows']
        for r in rows:
            by_customer[str(r['customer_id'])]=float(promo)
        if rows:
            counts[(cyc,plan,promo)]=len(rows)
print('Coupon target counts',counts,'total',len(by_customer))
print('Set promo', nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=by_group, by_group_plan=by_group_plan, by_customer=by_customer))
lead_by_group={'S2':10.0,'D_S07':10.0,'D_S04':20.0,'S3':50.0,'S1':20.0,'D_S03':20.0,'D_S05':20.0,'D_S06':20.0,'D_S10':20.0,
               'E1':0.0,'E2':0.0,'E3':0.0,'D_E02':0.0,'D_E07':0.0,'D_E08':0.0,'D_E09':0.0,'D_E01':0.0,'D_E03':0.0,'D_E05':0.0,'D_E10':0.0,'D_S08':20.0}
print('Lead promo', nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=lead_by_group, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={}))
# 4) Social: answer D_S07 accuracy/community concerns with concrete shipped evals and support metrics.
post="BI update: reasoning upgrade is live. We added reproducible column-ref + dashboard-logic evals, dated benchmark notebooks, and owners on every active ticket. Last 7d: no outage and active-customer tickets stayed same-day. Judge us on the numbers."
try:
    print('Post length',len(post))
    print('Social', nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=post, reply_to_post_id=1114))
except Exception as e:
    print('Social post skipped/error',e)
# 5) Enterprise inbox: reply to all latest non-agent/system leads. Offers are deliberately very low month-to-month to test whether conversion is price/friction constrained; per-seat compute is low enough to stay positive.
price_map={
    'E1': (1.5,3.0,5.0),
    'E2': (3.0,6.0,10.0),
    'E3': (5.0,9.0,15.0),
    'D_E01': (5.0,9.0,15.0),
    'D_E02': (2.0,4.0,7.0),
    'D_E03': (5.0,9.0,15.0),
    'D_E05': (4.0,7.0,12.0),
    'D_E07': (5.0,9.0,15.0),
    'D_E08': (4.0,7.0,12.0),
    'D_E09': (2.5,5.0,8.0),
    'D_E10': (2.0,4.0,7.0),
}
rows=nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
lt AS (SELECT e.* FROM enterprise_turns e JOIN latest l ON e.customer_id=l.customer_id AND e.message_id=l.mid)
SELECT lt.customer_id, lt.message_id, lt.day, lt.sender, lt.thread_type, lt.seat_count, c.group_id
FROM lt JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE lt.sender!='agent'
ORDER BY lt.message_id
""")['rows']
print('Enterprise latest non-agent rows',len(rows),'seats',sum((r.get('seat_count') or 0) for r in rows))
deals=[]; dist={}
for r in rows:
    g=r['group_id']; A,B,C=price_map.get(g,(3.0,6.0,10.0)); seats=r.get('seat_count') or 0
    if seats>=1000:
        B=max(2.0,B*0.85); C=max(3.0,C*0.85)
    deals.append([int(r['customer_id']), [['A',round(A,2)], ['B',round(B,2)], ['C',round(C,2)]]])
    dist[g]=dist.get(g,0)+1
print('Enterprise group dist',dist)
processed=0; errors=0
for i in range(0,len(deals),400):
    batch=deals[i:i+400]
    if not batch: continue
    res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=batch)
    processed += len(batch)
    status_counts={}
    for rr in res.get('results',[]):
        if not rr.get('success'): errors += 1
        key=rr.get('status') or rr.get('error')
        status_counts[key]=status_counts.get(key,0)+1
    print('Enterprise batch',i//400+1,'size',len(batch),'status',status_counts)
print('Enterprise processed',processed,'errors',errors)
# Verification
print('Latest open sender counts', nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
lt AS (SELECT e.* FROM enterprise_turns e JOIN latest l ON e.customer_id=l.customer_id AND e.message_id=l.mid)
SELECT sender,thread_type,COUNT(*) n,MIN(day) min_day,MAX(day) max_day FROM lt GROUP BY sender,thread_type ORDER BY sender,thread_type
""")['rows'])
# Verify promo by_customer count in latest settings
promo_cfg=nm.query("SELECT id,settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type='promotion' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1")['rows'][0]
try:
    data=json.loads(promo_cfg['settings_json']); print('Promo override id',promo_cfg['id'],'by_customer_count',len(data.get('by_customer',{})))
except Exception as e: print('promo parse err',e)
print('Ending cash',nm.query('SELECT SUM(amount) cash FROM ledger')['rows'][0]['cash'])

import novamind_api as nm, json, math
D=nm.vars.current_day
print('DAY',D)
# Ledger week net/daily
print('\nLedger totals last7')
for row in nm.query(f"""
SELECT category,SUM(amount) amount,COUNT(*) cnt FROM ledger WHERE day BETWEEN {D-6} AND {D} GROUP BY category ORDER BY amount
""")['rows']: print(row)
print('net', nm.query(f"SELECT SUM(amount) net FROM ledger WHERE day BETWEEN {D-6} AND {D}")['rows'][0]['net'])
print('\nDaily net last14')
for row in nm.query(f"""
SELECT day,SUM(amount) net,
 SUM(CASE WHEN category='subscription_payment' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) sub,
 SUM(CASE WHEN category='compute' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) compute,
 SUM(CASE WHEN category='operations' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) ops,
 SUM(CASE WHEN category='development' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) dev,
 SUM(CASE WHEN category='advertising' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) ads
 FROM ledger WHERE day BETWEEN {D-13} AND {D} GROUP BY day ORDER BY day
""")['rows']: print(row)
# Active book summary
print('\nActive summary by group/plan')
for row in nm.query("""
SELECT c.group_id,c.customer_type,s.plan,COUNT(*) subs,SUM(s.seat_count) seats,SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) mrr,AVG(s.effective_price) avg_eff,AVG(s.promotion) avg_promo
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id,c.customer_type,s.plan ORDER BY mrr DESC
""")['rows']: print(row)
# Status counts by customers/subs
print('\nSubscription status counts')
for row in nm.query("SELECT status,plan,COUNT(*) n,SUM(seat_count) seats,SUM(effective_price*seat_count) eff FROM subscriptions GROUP BY status,plan ORDER BY status,plan")['rows']: print(row)
# Cancellations aggregate last7 small only
print('\nSmall cancel aggregate last7')
for row in nm.query(f"""
SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,s.start_day,s.status,COUNT(*) n,AVG(s.effective_price) avg_eff,AVG(s.promotion) avg_promo
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.customer_type='small' AND s.end_day BETWEEN {D-6} AND {D}
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,s.start_day,s.status
ORDER BY c.group_id,s.start_day,s.plan,s.status
""")['rows'][:1000]: print(row)
print('\nS2/D_S07 cohort outcomes for dues 351-357')
starts=[]
for due in range(D-6,D+1):
  for k in range(1,13): starts.append(due-30*k)
inlist=','.join(map(str,sorted(set(starts))))
for group in ['S2','D_S07']:
 print('GROUP',group)
 for row in nm.query(f"""
 SELECT s.start_day, (({D}-s.start_day)/30) AS cycles_num, s.plan,s.status,COUNT(*) n,SUM(s.effective_price) eff_sum,AVG(s.effective_price) avg_eff,AVG(s.promotion) avg_promo
 FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
 WHERE c.group_id='{group}' AND s.start_day IN ({inlist})
 GROUP BY s.start_day,s.plan,s.status ORDER BY s.start_day,s.plan,s.status
 """)['rows']: print(row)
# Cohort outcomes condensed retention ratios by next cycle, for S2
print('\nS2 due351-357 retention by next cycle/plan')
for group in ['S2','D_S07']:
 print('GROUP',group)
 for row in nm.query(f"""
 WITH x AS (
   SELECT s.start_day, s.plan,s.status,COUNT(*) n,AVG(s.promotion) avg_promo,AVG(s.effective_price) avg_eff,
          CAST(ROUND(({D}-s.start_day)/30.0,0) AS INT) AS cycles
   FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
   WHERE c.group_id='{group}' AND s.start_day IN ({inlist})
   GROUP BY s.start_day,s.plan,s.status
 )
 SELECT cycles,plan,status,SUM(n) n,AVG(avg_promo) avg_promo,AVG(avg_eff) avg_eff FROM x GROUP BY cycles,plan,status ORDER BY cycles,plan,status
 """)['rows']: print(row)
# Upcoming due by groups/plans/cycles next 7/28
for horizon in [7,28]:
 print(f'\nUpcoming due next {horizon} days by group plan cycle')
 rows=nm.query(f"""
 WITH active AS (
  SELECT c.group_id,c.customer_type,s.plan,s.customer_id,s.seat_count,s.effective_price,s.promotion,s.start_day,
         CAST(({D}-s.start_day)/30 AS INT) AS cycles_elapsed,
         CASE WHEN ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({D} % 30) + 30) % 30)=0 THEN 30 ELSE ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({D} % 30) + 30) % 30) END AS days_to_due
  FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status='subscribed')
 SELECT group_id,customer_type,plan,cycles_elapsed+1 AS next_cycle,COUNT(*) n,SUM(seat_count) seats,SUM(effective_price*seat_count) current_eff_mrr,AVG(effective_price) avg_eff,AVG(promotion) avg_promo,
        MIN({D}+days_to_due) first_due,MAX({D}+days_to_due) last_due
 FROM active WHERE {D}+days_to_due BETWEEN {D+1} AND {D+horizon}
 GROUP BY group_id,customer_type,plan,next_cycle ORDER BY group_id,plan,next_cycle
 """)['rows']
 for row in rows: print(row)
 print('TOTAL', {'n':sum(r['n'] for r in rows), 'seats':sum(r['seats'] or 0 for r in rows), 'mrr_due':sum(r['current_eff_mrr'] or 0 for r in rows)})
# Upcoming due by day
print('\nDue next 28 by day')
for row in nm.query(f"""
WITH active AS (SELECT seat_count,effective_price,CASE WHEN ((billing_day_mod30 - ({D}%30)+30)%30)=0 THEN 30 ELSE ((billing_day_mod30-({D}%30)+30)%30) END days_to_due FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed')
SELECT {D}+days_to_due due_day, COUNT(*) n, SUM(seat_count) seats, SUM(effective_price*seat_count) eff_mrr FROM active WHERE {D}+days_to_due BETWEEN {D+1} AND {D+28} GROUP BY due_day ORDER BY due_day
""")['rows']: print(row)
# Issues
print('\nOpen issues active/substatus age')
for row in nm.query("""
SELECT i.group_id,CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN 'active' ELSE COALESCE(s.status,'no_sub') END sub_status,COUNT(*) issues,AVG(i.days_open) avg_age,MAX(i.days_open) max_age,SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open>=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ge1,SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open>=2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ge2
FROM issues i LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY i.group_id,sub_status ORDER BY issues DESC
""")['rows']: print(row)
print('\nActive issue buckets')
for row in nm.query("""
SELECT i.group_id,s.plan,i.days_open,COUNT(*) n FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed' WHERE i.status='open' GROUP BY i.group_id,s.plan,i.days_open ORDER BY i.group_id,s.plan,i.days_open
""")['rows']: print(row)
print('\nIssues opened/resolved last14')
for row in nm.query(f"""
WITH RECURSIVE days(day) AS (SELECT {D-13} UNION ALL SELECT day+1 FROM days WHERE day<{D}),
o AS (SELECT open_day day,COUNT(*) opened FROM issues WHERE open_day BETWEEN {D-13} AND {D} GROUP BY open_day),
r AS (SELECT resolved_day day,COUNT(*) resolved FROM issues WHERE resolved_day BETWEEN {D-13} AND {D} GROUP BY resolved_day)
SELECT days.day,COALESCE(o.opened,0) opened,COALESCE(r.resolved,0) resolved FROM days LEFT JOIN o USING(day) LEFT JOIN r USING(day) ORDER BY days.day
""")['rows']: print(row)
